question_text: Which of the following best explains why a solid cannot be easily compressed?

options.label: A
options.text: Solid particles are already very tightly packed together

options.label: B
options.text: Solids contain very heavy particles that resist movement

options.label: C
options.text: Solids have a very low temperature so particles cannot move

options.label: D
options.text: Solids are compressed during manufacture and cannot compress further

explanation: Solid particles are already packed as closely together as possible due to strong forces between them. There is very little space left, so it is extremely difficult to compress a solid any further.
